column1: # Ordered by hardware tier. This catalog is for MLX-capable Apple Silicon workflows, not Ollama.
# Recommendations are candidates only; the macOS profile script does not inspect installed MLX model files.

# The Qwen 3.5 9B entry below has passed a local MLX endpoint, Continue CLI workflows, and one VSCodium Continue Agent generated-Python scoped edit on Apple Silicon. It is not full language-matrix or real-project evidence.

High|mlx-community/Qwen3.5-9B-OptiQ-4bit|Use as the evidence-backed Apple Silicon MLX starting point before considering larger candidates that fit unified memory.|The bounded validation covers a local endpoint, Continue CLI generated-sample workflows, and one VSCodium Python scoped edit; validate any larger model and every intended surface independently.

Medium|mlx-community/Qwen3.5-9B-OptiQ-4bit|Validated for Continue CLI workflows and one VSCodium generated-Python scoped edit on Apple Silicon through a local OpenAI-compatible MLX endpoint.|Use only with a current MLX runtime, bind the server to 127.0.0.1, and repeat validation for every editor version, language, and real repository before relying on editor Agent mode.

Low|mlx-community/Qwen3.5-4B-4bit|Use for targeted read-only discovery and disposable one-file write smoke checks on constrained Apple Silicon machines.|The candidate passed a loopback endpoint structured-tool call plus Continue CLI read and disposable scoped-write checks; editor, plan, review, and full-matrix readiness are not claimed.
